elements.label: sec_1
elements.text:
Third Meeting of the Governing Council of National
Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF) held today
under the Chairmanship of Finance Minister, Shri


Arun Jaitley;

elements.label: para
elements.text:
FM reviews the performance of NIIF so far and road
ahead; A first close of NIIF is expected very soon as
far as fund raising is concerned.

elements.label: para
elements.text: Posted On: 14 JUL 2017 4:19PM by PIB Delhi

elements.label: para
elements.text:
The 24th Meeting of the Governing Council of the "National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIF)" was held 
here today under the Chairmanship of the Union Finance Minister, Shri Arun Jaitley where in the Finance Minister 
reviewed the performance of NIF so far and action taken by it in order to attract investment from foreign Soverign 
Wealth Funds among others in the infrastructure sector in the country.

elements.label: list
elements.text:
(i) Strong investment pipeline with investment opportunities is under consideration in the roads, ports, aviation
and power sectors.
(ii) NIF is Considering investments in third party managed funds focused on clean energy(Green Growth Fund
already committed), affordable housing and medium sized infrastructure companies

elements.label: para
elements.text:
The Governing Council expressed its desire for NIF to function as a major platform in India, to leverage and
attract overseas long-term investments in various infrastructure sectors in the Country.

elements.label: para
elements.text: As far as the issue of the Fund raising is concerned, it was informed that:

elements.label: list
elements.text:
(i) Master Contribution Agreement has been signed between Secretary, Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry
of Finance, Government of India (As the "Contributor") and National Investment and Infrastructure Fund Limited
(NIIFL) ( As the "Investment Manager"). The Agreement has been executed to formalize the commitment of INR
20,000 crores by Government of India to National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF).
(ii) Following this Agreement, steady progress on fund-raising efforts with several international institutional
investors, following-up on MoUs signed by Govt of India has been made. A first close of NIIF is expected very
soon.
(iii) The NIIF Governance Structure has been developed with technical assistance from DFID, UK which has been
further reviewed and confirmed by Stanford University's Global Project Centre under the MOU with the US
Treasury.

elements.label: para
elements.text: As far as the Operational Status of NIF is concerned, it was informed that :

elements.label: para
elements.text:
( i ) A team of twelve staff, including the CEO, National Investment and Infrastructure Fund Limited is now in
place. Further recruitments of high quality staff through a global search process is underway. Strong international
response to hiring efforts and advertisements for recruitment of expert staff has been received.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
(ii) The establishment of NIF's permanent Delhi office is complete and the establishment of its head office in
Mumbai is currently under refurbishment. NIF staff are already operating in a hired space.

elements.label: para
elements.text:
Shri Subhash Chandra Garg, Secretary, Department of Economic Affairs (DEA), Ms Anjuly Chib Duggal,
Secretary, Department of Financial Services (DFS), Shri T.V. Mohandas Pai and Shri Hemodia Kothari, Members
of the Governing Council and other senior officials were present during the aforesaid meeting.
